header.label: BLOG
header.title: Blog
header.subtitle: Notes on agentic workflows, static publishing, documentation, project maintenance, AI industry topics, and recent papers.

items.title: Agents need receipts
items.subtitle: Agent-assisted work needs source files, review gates, and visible decisions.
items.summary: A short note about agent-assisted development, evidence, and project memory.

blocks.lead: Agent-assisted work is only useful when the result can be inspected.
paragraphs: The useful part is not a transcript or a confident summary. It is the committed file, the validation output, the review note, and the decision that can be revisited later.
That is why this project keeps workflow files, state files, public artifacts, and review gates together. The site should explain the work, but the repository should prove it.
A good agentic workflow leaves receipts: scoped tasks, source links, reviewer verdicts, test results, and a small enough change set for a human to understand.

items.title: Static sites as workbenches
items.subtitle: Plain files can still behave like a serious publishing surface.
items.summary: A note on using static sites for technical work without turning maintenance into a build-system hobby.

blocks.lead: A static site does not need to become a pile of copied pages.
paragraphs: Plain HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and JSON can be enough when the site has one shell, one route registry, and reusable components.
The maintainable boundary is simple: content files describe the work; components describe reusable structures; the shell owns navigation, theme, and routing.
That keeps deployment boring and updates human-sized. Adding a project or article should be a data change, not a new folder full of duplicated markup.

items.title: Sprint review
items.subtitle: Cleaning project state, public routes, site copy, and repository alignment.
items.summary: A short public review note for AgenticCareerBoost site and workflow cleanup.

blocks.lead: The sprint tightened the public site around source-backed work.
paragraphs: The target was a site that a human can inspect and maintain: fewer duplicated pages, clearer data boundaries, and public status that comes from committed files.
AgenticCareerBoost now has a visible project dashboard in the project flow, and the site runtime renders content from structured sources rather than page copies.
The remaining standard is simple: every future public section should be created by adding data and assets, not by copying a page.
